description: Port dataset_preparation/: videos_to_tfrecords.py -> videos to PyTorch-compatible format (e.g., image folders, HDF5, or WebDataset). images_to_tfrecords.py -> similar conversion. download_pouring_data.sh -> keep as shell script. visualize_tfrecords.py -> visualize PyTorch datasets. Target: src/tcc/dataset_preparation/
title: Port dataset preparation utilities

description:
## Goal

Create Mermaid diagrams documenting the TCC codebase architecture. Place them in `docs/`.

## Diagrams to create

1. **Module dependency diagram** — Show how the top-level modules relate: `train.py`, `evaluate.py`, `extract_embeddings.py`, `visualize_alignment.py` → `algorithms.py` → `algos/*`, `models.py`, `datasets.py`, `config.py`, `tcc/*`, `evaluation/*`, `preprocessors/*`, `dataset_preparation/*`

2. **Training pipeline flowchart** — Data loading (TFRecords → `datasets.py` → `preprocessors/`) → model forward pass (`models.py` ResNetV2-50) → algorithm loss (`algos/*` + `tcc/*`) → optimization

3. **Evaluation pipeline flowchart** — Extract embeddings → SVM training → evaluation tasks (classification, few-shot, event completion, Kendall's Tau)

4. **TCC loss computation** — Detail the cycle-consistency loss: `tcc/alignment.py` (compute_alignment_loss), `tcc/deterministic_alignment.py` (align_pair_of_sequences), `tcc/stochastic_alignment.py`, `tcc/losses.py`

## Output

- `docs/architecture.md` — All diagrams with brief explanatory text
- Use Mermaid syntax compatible with GitHub rendering
title: Draft architecture Mermaid diagrams for the TCC repo

description: Port models.py: BaseModel (ResNet50V2 -> torchvision.models.resnet50, extract Conv4c features 14x14x1024), ConvEmbedder (Conv3D context layers, 3D max pooling, FC 512->512->128, L2 normalize), ConvGRUEmbedder (Conv2D + GRU), Classifier (FC layers with dropout). Helper functions: get_model(), get_conv_bn_layers(), get_fc_layers(). Target: src/tcc/models.py. Key mappings: tf.keras.Model->nn.Module, tf.keras.layers.Conv3D->nn.Conv3d, CuDNNGRU->nn.GRU, keras regularizers->weight_decay in optimizer.
title: Port model architecture to PyTorch

description: Port evaluation/: classification.py (SVM on embeddings, per-frame accuracy), few_shot_classification.py (1-shot SVM), event_completion.py (linear regression, R2 score), kendalls_tau.py (nearest-neighbor temporal alignment, tau statistic). Port evaluate.py main loop: load checkpoints, extract embeddings, run eval tasks, log metrics. Target: src/tcc/evaluation/
title: Port evaluation tasks to PyTorch

description:
## Context

The **tcc** (Temporal Cycle-Consistency Learning) repo is a CVPR 2019 self-supervised video representation learning codebase. It currently has:
- A bare `requirements.txt` with no modern packaging
- TensorFlow 1.x-era code (uses `tf.compat.v1`, TFRecords, ResNetV2-50)
- No containerization or reproducible dev environment

## Goal

Port the **uv + Docker devcontainer infrastructure** from `eng-ai-agents` (`/home/pantelis.monogioudis/local/web/sites/courses/course-containers/eng-ai-agents`) to this repo, using the **pytorch dev GPU** container variant as the base.

## Reference Architecture (eng-ai-agents)

The eng-ai-agents repo uses a "container-first, uv-managed, system-site-packages bridge" pattern:

1. **Dockerfile** (`docker/Dockerfile.torch.dev.gpu`):
   - Base: `pytorch/pytorch:2.7.1-cuda12.8-cudnn9-runtime`
   - Creates `vscode` non-root user (UID 1001) with sudo
   - Installs system deps: curl, git-lfs, ffmpeg, make, bash-completion, python3-venv, openssh-client
   - uv installed via `COPY --from=ghcr.io/astral-sh/uv:latest /uv /uvx /bin/`
   - Generates `/etc/pip/constraint.txt` from `pip list --format=freeze` to pin base-image packages
   - Installs Node.js 18.x, Quarto, 1Password CLI

2. **docker-compose.yml**:
   - Service `torch.dev.gpu` with NVIDIA GPU reservation (all GPUs)
   - Repo mounted at `/workspaces/<project>`
   - SSH agent forwarding, `.env` for secrets
   - Ports: Jupyter 8888, Quarto 4100, dev 8000
   - `.venv/bin` prepended to PATH, VIRTUAL_ENV, UV_CACHE_DIR env vars

3. **devcontainer.json**:
   - References docker-compose, default service `torch.dev.gpu`
   - Sets `UV_LINK_MODE=copy`, `UV_PROJECT_ENVIRONMENT`
   - Post-create checks for `.venv`
   - VS Code extensions: Python, Pylance, Jupyter, Quarto, Ruff, etc.

4. **pyproject.toml**:
   - Build backend: hatchling
   - Python constraint: `>=3.11`
   - Core deps + optional groups: `[notebooks]`, `[dev]`
   - `[tool.uv]` dev-dependencies
   - ruff, mypy, pytest config

5. **Makefile** (key targets):
   - `venv`: `uv venv --python <PYTHON> --system-site-packages`
   - `install`: `uv pip install -e .`
   - `install-dev`, `install-notebooks`
   - `start`: full init (recreate venv, deps-sync, install, ipykernel)
   - `format`, `lint`, `test`, `deps-update`, `deps-sync`

6. **Key design**: `--system-site-packages` venv + constraint file ensures uv doesn't reinstall CUDA-optimized PyTorch from the base image.

## Tasks

- [ ] Create `pyproject.toml` with hatchling backend, project deps (adapt from `requirements.txt` + TF deps), optional groups `[dev]`, `[notebooks]`
- [ ] Create `docker/Dockerfile.torch.dev.gpu` based on eng-ai-agents template
- [ ] Create `docker-compose.yml` with `torch.dev.gpu` service (NVIDIA GPU, volume mounts, ports)
- [ ] Create `.devcontainer/devcontainer.json` for VS Code Dev Containers
- [ ] Create `Makefile` with venv, install, start, format, lint, test targets
- [ ] Generate `uv.lock` from pyproject.toml
- [ ] Add `.env.example` documenting required env vars
- [ ] Remove legacy `requirements.txt` (or keep as reference)
- [ ] Test the full workflow: build image → start container → make start → run training
title: Introduce uv and Docker infrastructure (pytorch dev GPU)

description: Port algos/: algorithm.py base class (tf.keras.Model->nn.Module, train_one_iter with autograd), alignment.py (TCC algorithm wrapping core losses), sal.py (Shuffle-and-Learn with triplet sampling and FC classifier), tcn.py (N-pairs metric loss on anchor/positive embeddings), classification.py (supervised baseline with FC classifier), alignment_sal_tcn.py (combined losses with configurable weights). Port algorithms.py registry (get_algo factory). Target: src/tcc/algos/
title: Port algorithm classes to PyTorch

description: Port tcc/ directory: alignment.py (compute_alignment_loss), deterministic_alignment.py (pairwise_l2_distance, get_scaled_similarity, align_pair_of_sequences), stochastic_alignment.py (gen_cycles, _align_single_cycle), losses.py (classification_loss, regression_loss with MSE/MSE+var/Huber variants). Target: src/tcc/losses/ or src/tcc/alignment/. Key TF-to-PyTorch mappings: tf.nn.softmax->F.softmax, tf.matmul->torch.matmul, tf.nn.l2_normalize->F.normalize, tf.stop_gradient->detach(), tf.keras.losses.categorical_crossentropy->F.cross_entropy. Unit tests for each function.
title: Port TCC core losses to PyTorch

description:
## Goal

Establish branch strategy for the TF-to-PyTorch migration:

1. **Create `tf2` branch** from current `main` — preserves the existing Keras/TF2 codebase as-is
2. **Reserve `main`** for the PyTorch translation of the TCC codebase

## Steps

- [ ] Create `tf2` branch from current HEAD of `main`
- [ ] Push `tf2` branch to origin
- [ ] Clear TF-specific source code from `main` (keep infrastructure: CLAUDE.md, .beads, docs/, etc.)
- [ ] Update CLAUDE.md to reflect the PyTorch migration strategy
- [ ] Update README.md to note the branch structure: `tf2` = original TF code, `main` = PyTorch port

## Branch structure

- `main` — PyTorch implementation (target)
- `tf2` — Original Keras/TF2 code (archived reference)
title: Create tf2 branch and reserve main for PyTorch translation

description: Port datasets.py: Replace TFRecords with PyTorch Dataset/DataLoader. decode() -> custom Dataset class reading video frames. preprocess_input() -> torchvision transforms (resize 224x224, normalize, augmentations). sample_and_preprocess() -> custom sampling logic (stride, offset_uniform strategies). create_dataset() -> DataLoader with num_workers, pin_memory. Support for Pouring and Penn Action datasets. Handle TCN special double-sampling for anchor/positive pairs. Target: src/tcc/datasets.py
title: Port dataset loading to PyTorch

description: Port config.py: Replace EasyDict with Python dataclasses or OmegaConf. Sections: TRAIN (max_iters, batch_size, num_frames), EVAL (batch_size, tasks, kendalls_tau_stride), MODEL (embedder_type, base_model, conv_embedder_model, embedding_size=128), ALIGNMENT (cycle_length, temperature, loss_type, similarity_type, stochastic_matching, variance_lambda), SAL (dropout, fc_layers, shuffle_fraction), TCN (positive_window, reg_lambda), OPTIMIZER (type, lr schedule), DATA (sampling_strategy, stride, augmentation). Support YAML config files. Target: src/tcc/config.py
title: Port configuration system

description: Port train.py: Replace tf.distribute.MirroredStrategy with PyTorch DDP or DataParallel. Training loop: DataLoader iteration, forward pass, loss.backward(), optimizer.step(). LR scheduling (fixed, exp_decay, manual). Checkpointing with torch.save/load. TensorBoard logging via torch.utils.tensorboard. Gradient accumulation support. Target: src/tcc/train.py
title: Port training loop to PyTorch

description:
## Goal

Port the entire TCC codebase from the `tf2` branch (Keras/TF2) to PyTorch on `main`. Test execution in the `torch.dev.gpu` devcontainer.

## Source Reference

All original code lives on the `tf2` branch. Key modules to port:

### Core TCC Library (`tcc/`)
- `tcc/alignment.py` — `compute_alignment_loss` (main TCC loss)
- `tcc/deterministic_alignment.py` — `align_pair_of_sequences`, deterministic alignment loss
- `tcc/stochastic_alignment.py` — stochastic alignment variant
- `tcc/losses.py` — shared loss utilities

### Algorithms (`algos/`)
- `algos/alignment.py` — TCC algorithm
- `algos/sal.py` — Shuffle and Learn
- `algos/tcn.py` — Time-Contrastive Networks
- `algos/alignment_sal_tcn.py` — combined methods
- `algos/classification.py` — supervised baseline
- `algos/algorithm.py` — base algorithm class

### Models & Data
- `models.py` — ResNetV2-50 backbone (replace with torchvision ResNet50)
- `datasets.py` — TFRecord loading → PyTorch Dataset/DataLoader
- `preprocessors/` — sequence preprocessing → torchvision transforms
- `config.py` — ml_collections config → YAML/dataclass config

### Training & Evaluation
- `train.py` — training loop → PyTorch training loop
- `evaluate.py` — evaluation entry point
- `evaluation/` — all evaluation tasks (classification, few-shot, event completion, Kendall's Tau)
- `extract_embeddings.py` — embedding extraction
- `visualize_alignment.py` — visualization

### Data Preparation
- `dataset_preparation/` — video/image to dataset conversion (TFRecords → PyTorch format)

## Porting Strategy

1. Port `tcc/` core losses first (pure math, minimal framework dependency)
2. Port `models.py` (swap ResNetV2-50 for torchvision resnet50)
3. Port `datasets.py` (TFRecords → PyTorch Dataset with video loading)
4. Port `algos/` (algorithms using ported losses + models)
5. Port `train.py` and `evaluate.py` (training/eval loops)
6. Port `evaluation/` tasks
7. Port `dataset_preparation/` utilities

## Testing

- All code must run inside the `torch.dev.gpu` devcontainer
- Unit tests for each ported module in `tests/`
- Verify GPU acceleration works: `torch.cuda.is_available()`
- Smoke test: full training on pouring dataset
title: Translate TF2 codebase to PyTorch
